goal: Use Loop to execute DEV-015 for xtgzpt: implement the full human approval closure after DEV-014. The approval workflow must support approval instances, nodes, current approver, approve/reject/return/transfer/add-sign actions, source object state writeback, audit records, workbench integration, permissions, and a production-oriented 1440 desktop page experience. Loop must run autonomously without asking the user between stages while useful output remains possible.
acceptance: Implementation is executed through the codex_executor connector in the isolated Loop worktree, not by manual chat edits.
Loop loads the full xtgzpt project context from docs/loop/00_MEMORY_INDEX.md and records loaded project memory files.

Loop reads PROJECT_INTAKE.md, README.md, docs/PROJECT_CONSTITUTION.md, docs/TECHNICAL_STANDARD.md, docs/TEST_ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD.md, docs/dev-readiness/02_PERMISSION_MATRIX.md, docs/dev-readiness/03_OBJECTS_AND_STATE_MACHINES.md, docs/dev-readiness/04_API_DRAFT.md, docs/dev-readiness/06_AUDIT_LOG_MATRIX.md, docs/dev-readiness/08_TEST_CASES_AND_ACCEPTANCE.md, docs/dev-readiness/10_DEVELOPMENT_BACKLOG.md, docs/dev-log/DEV-014.md, docs/dev-log/AUDIT-014.md, and latest docs/loop/runtime-memory records before implementation.
Loop writes project-level action history to xtgzpt docs/loop/runtime-memory.
DEV-015 and AUDIT-015 records are written to docs/dev-log.

Approval instances can be created from supported source objects without implementing finance, procurement, inventory, ERP, mobile, external notifications, external OCR, or external signature services.
Contract approval handoff from DEV-014 can create or enter a real approval instance without bypassing second review and human risk confirmation rules.
Approval instances record source object type/id, organization, initiator, title, status, current node, current approver, created time, updated time, and history.
Approval nodes support pending, processing, approved, rejected, returned, transferred, and add_signed states with human-only approvers.
Approve, reject, return, transfer, and add-sign actions require approval permission and current-node handler validation on the backend.
AI cannot be an approver and cannot automatically approve, reject, return, transfer, add-sign, or write final approval decisions.
Approval result writes back to supported source objects, including contract status: approved when the approval finishes approved, rejected/returned when the approval is rejected or returned.
All approval submission, node decision, transfer, add-sign, return, reject, approve, permission denial, and source writeback actions write audit records.
Workbench and system notifications include pending approvals and approval status changes for the current user.
Unauthorized users cannot read approval details, source sensitive fields, comments, node handlers, or audit context outside their data scope.

Frontend uses the existing Approval top-level menu only and provides a complete production-style 1440 desktop approval workspace:待我审批, 我发起的, 审批详情, source summary, node timeline, current handler, action panel, history, empty/loading/no-permission/error states.
UI acceptance targets a single 1440 desktop layout that remains usable on ordinary computer screens; do not spend scope on separate 1280/960/mobile variants in DEV-015.

Loop continues automatically across implementation, repair, review, verification, PR preparation, and next useful execution planning; do not ask the user for per-stage confirmation unless blocked by missing credentials, missing secrets, production risk, or contradictory requirements.
Loop runs xtgzpt npm gates: lint, typecheck, test, build, smoke:api, npm audit --audit-level=low, and git diff --check where available.
Any failure is classified, retried or repaired according to Loop policy, and written to intent debt or repair queue if not resolved.

codex_command: You are the Loop writer executor for xtgzpt DEV-015. Work only in the current working tree. Read docs/loop/00_MEMORY_INDEX.md, PROJECT_INTAKE.md, README.md, docs/PROJECT_CONSTITUTION.md, docs/TECHNICAL_STANDARD.md, docs/TEST_ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD.md, docs/dev-readiness/02_PERMISSION_MATRIX.md, docs/dev-readiness/03_OBJECTS_AND_STATE_MACHINES.md, docs/dev-readiness/04_API_DRAFT.md, docs/dev-readiness/06_AUDIT_LOG_MATRIX.md, docs/dev-readiness/08_TEST_CASES_AND_ACCEPTANCE.md, docs/dev-readiness/10_DEVELOPMENT_BACKLOG.md, docs/dev-log/DEV-014.md, docs/dev-log/AUDIT-014.md, and recent docs/loop/runtime-memory records before editing. Complete DEV-015 approval closure. Required outcome: real human approval instances, nodes, current approver, approve/reject/return/transfer/add-sign actions, backend permission and current handler checks, source object writeback especially contract approval handoff/status, workbench/notification integration, audit records, permission-safe detail APIs, PostgreSQL-compatible migration assets, frontend approval workspace inside the existing Approval menu only, and DEV-015/AUDIT-015 docs plus runtime memory. UI target is one production-oriented 1440 desktop page layout that remains usable on ordinary computer screens; do not spend this phase on separate 1280/960/mobile variants. Loop is authorized to continue without user confirmation between stages while useful output remains possible. Keep AI human-boundary rules: AI cannot approve, reject, return, transfer, add-sign, become an approver, sign, pay, or confirm execution. Do not implement finance, procurement, inventory, ERP, external OCR, external e-signature, external notifications, production writes, deployment, secrets, mobile app, new top-level menu, or unrelated features.
